G1525.brief: I go in, come in, enter
G1525.full: I go in, come in, enter.

G3825.brief: again, further, on the other hand
G3825.full: again, back, once more, further, on the other hand.

G1519.full: into, in, unto, to, upon, towards, for, among.

G1223.full: (a) gen: through, throughout, by the instrumentality of, (b) acc: through, on account of, by reason of, for the sake of, because of.

G2250.full: a day, the period from sunrise to sunset.

G191.full: I hear, listen, comprehend by hearing; pass: is heard, reported.

G3754.full: that, since, because; may introduce direct discourse.

G3624.full: (a) a house, the material building, (b) a household, family, lineage, nation.

G4863.full: I gather together, collect, assemble, receive with hospitality, entertain.

G5620.full: so that, therefore, so then, so as to.

G5562.full: (lit: I make room, hence) (a) I have room for, receive, contain, (b) I make room for by departing, go, make progress, turn myself.

G2980.full: (I talk, chatter in classical Greek, but in NT a more dignified word) I speak, say.

G846.brief: he, she, it, they, them, same
G846.full: he, she, it, they, them, same.

G3056.brief: a word, speech, divine utterance, analogy
G3056.full: a word, speech, divine utterance, analogy.

G5342.full: I carry, bear, bring; I conduct, lead; perhaps: I make publicly known.

G142.full: I raise, lift up, take away, remove.

G1410.full: (a) I am powerful, have (the) power, (b) I am able, I can.

G4374.full: (a) I bring to, (b) characteristically: I offer (of gifts, sacrifices, etc).

G3793.full: a crowd, mob, the common people.

G648.brief: I unroof, take the roof off
G648.full: I unroof, take the roof off.

G4721.brief: a flat roof of a house
G4721.full: a flat roof of a house.

G1846.full: (a) I dig out, hence: I open up, (b) I gouge.

G5465.full: I let down, lower, slacken, loosen.

G2895.full: a bed, mattress, mat of a poor man.

G2621.full: I recline (at table); more often: I keep my bed, am lying ill (in bed).

G2424.full: Jesus; the Greek form of Joshua; Jesus, son of Eliezer; Jesus, surnamed Justus.

G4102.full: faith, belief, trust, confidence; fidelity, faithfulness.

G3004.full: (denoting speech in progress), (a) I say, speak; I mean, mention, tell, (b) I call, name, especially in the pass., (c) I tell, command.

G863.brief: I send away, release, remit, forgive, permit
G863.full: (a) I send away, (b) I let go, release, permit to depart, (c) I remit, forgive, (d) I permit, suffer.

G266.full: prop: missing the mark; hence: (a) guilt, sin, (b) a fault, failure (in an ethical sense), sinful deed.

G1161.brief: but, on the other hand, and
G1161.full: a weak adversative particle, generally placed second in its clause; but, on the other hand, and.

G5100.full: any one, some one, a certain one or thing.

G1122.brief: a scribe, town-clerk, man of learning
G1122.full: (a) in Jerusalem, a scribe, one learned in the Jewish Law, a religious teacher, (b) at Ephesus, the town-clerk, the secretary of the city, (c) a man of learning generally.

G1563.full: (a) there, yonder, in that place, (b) thither, there.

G2521.full: I sit, am seated, enthroned; I dwell, reside.

G2588.full: lit: the heart; mind, character, inner self, will, intention, center.

G987.full: I speak evil against, blaspheme, use abusive or scurrilous language about (God or men).

G1921.brief: I come to know, discern, recognize
G1921.full: I come to know by directing my attention to him or it, I perceive, discern, recognize; aor: I found out.

G1453.full: (a) I wake, arouse, (b) I raise up.

G4043.full: I walk, hence Hebraistically (in an ethical sense): I conduct my life, live.

G1849.full: (a) power, authority, weight, especially: moral authority, influence, (b) in a quasi-personal sense, derived from later Judaism, of a spiritual power, and hence of an earthly power.

G444.brief: a man, one of the human race
G444.full: a man, one of the human race.

G1909.brief: on, to, against, on the basis of, at
G1909.full: on, to, against, on the basis of, at.

G1093.full: the earth, soil, land, region, country, inhabitants of a region.

G5217.full: I go away, depart, begone, die.

G1715.full: in front, before the face; sometimes made a subst. by the addition of the article: in front of, before the face of.

G3956.brief: all, the whole, every kind of
G3956.full: all, the whole, every kind of.

G1839.full: (lit: I remove from a standing position), (a) in trans. tenses: I astonish, amaze, (b) in intrans. tenses: I am astonished, amazed; I am out of my mind, am mad.

G1392.brief: I glorify, honor, bestow glory on
G1392.full: I glorify, honor, bestow glory on.

G3844.full: gen: from; dat: beside, in the presence of; acc: alongside of.

G2281.full: (a) the sea, in contrast to the land, (b) a particular sea or lake, e.g. the sea of Galilee (Tiberias), the Red Sea.

G3855.brief: I pass by, depart, pass away
G3855.full: I pass by, depart, pass away.

G3017.full: (Hebrew), Levi, (a) an ancestor of Jesus, (b) another ancestor of Jesus, (c) third son of Jacob, the patriarch, and founder of a tribe named after him, (d) son of Alphaeus, and called Matthew, a revenue officer and one of the twelve apostles of Jesus.

G256.full: Alphaeus; apparently two persons, (a) father of Levi, and (b) father of James.

G450.brief: I raise up, set up, rise from
G450.full: I raise up, set up; I rise from among (the) dead; I arise, appear.

G1096.brief: I come into being, am born
G1096.full: I come into being, am born, become, come about, happen.

G3614.full: a house, household, dwelling; meton: goods, property, means.

G5330.full: a Pharisee, one of the Jewish sect so called.

G2068.full: I eat, partake of food; met: I devour, consume (e.g. as rust does).

G3326.full: (a) gen: with, in company with, (b) acc: (1) behind, beyond, after, of place, (2) after, of time, with nouns, neut. of adjectives.

G2480.full: I have strength, am strong, am in full health and vigor, am able; meton: I prevail.

G2564.full: (a) I call, summon, invite, (b) I call, name.

G1342.full: just; especially, just in the eyes of God; righteous; the elect (a Jewish idea).

G2491.full: John: the Baptist, the apostle, a member of the Sanhedrin, or John Mark.

G3745.brief: how much, how great, how many
G3745.full: how much, how great, how many, as great as, as much.

G522.full: I take away, remove; pass: I am taken away, withdrawn.

G4470.full: a piece of cloth, a remnant torn off.

G2440.full: a long flowing outer garment, tunic.

G3820.full: old, ancient, not new or recent.

G4138.full: (a) a fill, fullness; full complement; supply, patch, supplement, (b) fullness, filling, fulfillment, completion.

G4978.full: a rent, as in a garment; a division, dissention.

G906.brief: I cast, throw, rush, put, place, drop
G906.full: (a) I cast, throw, rush, (b) often, in the weaker sense: I place, put, drop.

G4486.full: I rend, break asunder; I break forth (into speech); I throw or dash down.

G622.full: (a) I kill, destroy, (b) I lose, mid: I am perishing (the resultant death being viewed as certain).

G3899.full: I go past, pass by, pass along by.

G4160.full: (a) I make, manufacture, construct, (b) I do, act, cause.

G314.full: I read, know again, know certainly, recognize, discern.

G3983.full: I am hungry, needy, desire earnestly.

G4459.full: how, in what manner, by what means.

G8.full: Abiathar, a priest in King David's time.

G4286.full: a setting forth, the show-bread; predetermination, purpose.

G5315.full: I eat, partake of food; met: I devour, consume (e.g. as rust does); used only in fut. and 2nd aor. tenses.

G2409.full: a priest, one who offers sacrifice to a god (in Jewish and pagan religions; of Christians only met.).

G1325.full: I offer, give; I put, place.

bsb.1: A few days later Jesus went back to Capernaum. And when the people heard that He was home,
bsb.2: they gathered in such large numbers that there was no more room, not even outside the door, as Jesus spoke the word to them.
bsb.3: Then a paralytic was brought to Him, carried by four men.
bsb.4: Since they were unable to get to Jesus through the crowd, they uncovered the roof above Him, made an opening, and lowered the paralytic on his mat.
bsb.5: When Jesus saw their faith, He said to the paralytic, “Son, your sins are forgiven.”
bsb.6: But some of the scribes were sitting there and thinking in their hearts,
bsb.7: “Why does this man speak like this? He is blaspheming! Who can forgive sins but God alone?”
bsb.8: At once Jesus knew in His spirit that they were thinking this way within themselves. “Why are you thinking these things in your hearts?” He asked.
bsb.9: “Which is easier: to say to a paralytic, ‘Your sins are forgiven,’ or to say, ‘Get up, pick up your mat, and walk’?
bsb.10: But so that you may know that the Son of Man has authority on earth to forgive sins...” He said to the paralytic,
bsb.11: “I tell you, get up, pick up your mat, and go home.”
bsb.12: And immediately the man got up, picked up his mat, and walked out in front of them all. As a result, they were all astounded and glorified God, saying, “We have never seen anything like this!”
bsb.13: Once again Jesus went out beside the sea. All the people came to Him, and He taught them there.
bsb.14: As He was walking along, He saw Levi son of Alphaeus sitting at the tax booth. “Follow Me,” He told him, and Levi got up and followed Him.
bsb.15: While Jesus was dining at Levi’s house, many tax collectors and sinners were eating with Him and His disciples—for there were many who followed Him.
bsb.16: When the scribes who were Pharisees saw Jesus eating with these people, they asked His disciples, “Why does He eat with tax collectors and sinners?”
bsb.17: On hearing this, Jesus told them, “It is not the healthy who need a doctor, but the sick. I have not come to call the righteous, but sinners.”
bsb.18: Now John’s disciples and the Pharisees were often fasting. So people came to Jesus and asked, “Why don’t Your disciples fast like John’s disciples and those of the Pharisees?”
bsb.19: Jesus replied, “How can the guests of the bridegroom fast while He is with them? As long as He is with them, they cannot fast.
bsb.20: But the time will come when the bridegroom will be taken from them; then they will fast.
bsb.21: No one sews a patch of unshrunk cloth on an old garment. If he does, the new piece will pull away from the old, and a worse tear will result.
bsb.22: And no one pours new wine into old wineskins. If he does, the wine will burst the skins, and both the wine and the wineskins will be ruined. Instead, new wine is poured into new wineskins.”
bsb.23: One Sabbath Jesus was passing through the grainfields, and His disciples began to pick the heads of grain as they walked along.
bsb.24: So the Pharisees said to Him, “Look, why are they doing what is unlawful on the Sabbath?”
bsb.25: Jesus replied, “Have you never read what David did when he and his companions were hungry and in need?
bsb.26: During the high priesthood of Abiathar, he entered the house of God and ate the consecrated bread, which was lawful only for the priests. And he gave some to his companions as well.”
bsb.27: Then Jesus declared, “The Sabbath was made for man, not man for the Sabbath.
bsb.28: Therefore, the Son of Man is Lord even of the Sabbath.”

web.1: When he entered again into Capernaum after some days, it was heard that he was in the house.
web.2: Immediately many were gathered together, so that there was no more room, not even around the door; and he spoke the word to them.
web.3: Four people came, carrying a paralytic to him.
web.4: When they could not come near to him for the crowd, they removed the roof where he was. When they had broken it up, they let down the mat that the paralytic was lying on.
web.5: Jesus, seeing their faith, said to the paralytic, “Son, your sins are forgiven you.”
web.6: But there were some of the scribes sitting there, and reasoning in their hearts,
web.7: “Why does this man speak blasphemies like that? Who can forgive sins but God alone?”
web.8: Immediately Jesus, perceiving in his spirit that they so reasoned within themselves, said to them, “Why do you reason these things in your hearts?
web.9: Which is easier, to tell the paralytic, ‘Your sins are forgiven;’ or to say, ‘Arise, and take up your bed, and walk?’
web.10: But that you may know that the Son of Man has authority on earth to forgive sins”—he said to the paralytic—
web.11: “I tell you, arise, take up your mat, and go to your house.”
web.12: He arose, and immediately took up the mat, and went out in front of them all; so that they were all amazed, and glorified God, saying, “We never saw anything like this!”
web.13: He went out again by the seaside. All the multitude came to him, and he taught them.
web.14: As he passed by, he saw Levi, the son of Alphaeus, sitting at the tax office, and he said to him, “Follow me.” And he arose and followed him.
web.15: He was reclining at the table in his house, and many tax collectors and sinners sat down with Jesus and his disciples, for there were many, and they followed him.
web.16: The scribes and the Pharisees, when they saw that he was eating with the sinners and tax collectors, said to his disciples, “Why is it that he eats and drinks with tax collectors and sinners?”
web.17: When Jesus heard it, he said to them, “Those who are healthy have no need for a physician, but those who are sick. I came not to call the righteous, but sinners to repentance.”
web.18: John’s disciples and the Pharisees were fasting, and they came and asked him, “Why do John’s disciples and the disciples of the Pharisees fast, but your disciples don’t fast?”
web.19: Jesus said to them, “Can the groomsmen fast while the bridegroom is with them? As long as they have the bridegroom with them, they can’t fast.
web.20: But the days will come when the bridegroom will be taken away from them, and then will they fast in that day.
web.21: No one sews a piece of unshrunk cloth on an old garment, or else the patch shrinks and the new tears away from the old, and a worse hole is made.
web.22: No one puts new wine into old wineskins, or else the new wine will burst the skins, and the wine pours out, and the skins will be destroyed; but they put new wine into fresh wineskins.”
web.23: He was going on the Sabbath day through the grain fields, and his disciples began, as they went, to pluck the ears of grain.
web.24: The Pharisees said to him, “Behold, why do they do that which is not lawful on the Sabbath day?”
web.25: He said to them, “Did you never read what David did, when he had need, and was hungry—he, and those who were with him?
web.26: How he entered into God’s house at the time of Abiathar the high priest, and ate the show bread, which is not lawful to eat except for the priests, and gave also to those who were with him?”
web.27: He said to them, “The Sabbath was made for man, not man for the Sabbath.
web.28: Therefore the Son of Man is lord even of the Sabbath.”

asv.1: And when he entered again into Capernaum after some days, it was noised that he was in the house.
asv.2: And many were gathered together, so that there was no longer room [for them], no, not even about the door: and he spake the word unto them.
asv.3: And they come, bringing unto him a man sick of the palsy, borne of four.
asv.4: And when they could not come nigh unto him for the crowd, they uncovered the roof where he was: and when they had broken it up, they let down the bed whereon the sick of the palsy lay.
asv.5: And Jesus seeing their faith saith unto the sick of the palsy, Son, thy sins are forgiven.
asv.6: But there were certain of the scribes sitting there, and reasoning in their hearts,
asv.7: Why doth this man thus speak? he blasphemeth: who can forgive sins but one, [even] God?
asv.8: And straightway Jesus, perceiving in his spirit that they so reasoned within themselves, saith unto them, Why reason ye these things in your hearts?
asv.9: Which is easier, to say to the sick of the palsy, Thy sins are forgiven; or to say, Arise, and take up thy bed, and walk?
asv.10: But that ye may know that the Son of man hath authority on earth to forgive sins (he saith to the sick of the palsy),
asv.11: I say unto thee, Arise, take up thy bed, and go unto thy house.
asv.12: And he arose, and straightway took up the bed, and went forth before them all; insomuch that they were all amazed, and glorified God, saying, We never saw it on this fashion.
asv.13: And he went forth again by the sea side; and all the multitude resorted unto him, and he taught them.
asv.14: And as he passed by, he saw Levi the [son] of Alphaeus sitting at the place of toll, and he saith unto him, Follow me. And he arose and followed him.
asv.15: And it came to pass, that he was sitting at meat in his house, and many publicans and sinners sat down with Jesus and his disciples: for there were many, and they followed him.
asv.16: And the scribes of the Pharisees, when they saw that he was eating with the sinners and publicans, said unto his disciples, [How is it] that he eateth and drinketh with publicans and sinners?
asv.17: And when Jesus heard it, he saith unto them, They that are whole have no need of a physician, but they that are sick: I came not to call the righteous, but sinners.
asv.18: And John`s disciples and the Pharisees were fasting: and they come and say unto him, Why do John`s disciples and the disciples of the Pharisees fast, but thy disciples fast not?
asv.19: And Jesus said unto them, Can the sons of the bridechamber fast, while the bridegroom is with them? as long as they have the bridegroom with them, they cannot fast.
asv.20: But the days will come, when the bridegroom shall be taken away from them, and then will they fast in that day.
asv.21: No man seweth a piece of undressed cloth on an old garment: else that which should fill it up taketh from it, the new from the old, and a worse rent is made.
asv.22: And no man putteth new wine into old wineskins; else the wine will burst the skins, and the wine perisheth, and the skins: but [they put] new wine into fresh wine-skins.
asv.23: And it came to pass, that he was going on the sabbath day through the grainfields; and his disciples began, as they went, to pluck the ears.
asv.24: And the Pharisees said unto him, Behold, why do they on the sabbath day that which is not lawful?
asv.25: And he said unto them, Did ye never read what David did, when he had need, and was hungry, he, and they that were with him?
asv.26: How he entered into the house of God when Abiathar was high priest, and ate the showbread, which it is not lawful to eat save for the priests, and gave also to them that were with him?
asv.27: And he said unto them, The sabbath was made for man, and not man for the sabbath:
asv.28: so that the Son of man is lord even of the sabbath.

kjv.1: And again he entered into Capernaum, after some days; and it was noised that he was in the house.
kjv.2: And straightway many were gathered together, insomuch that there was no room to receive them, no, not so much as about the door: and he preached the word unto them.
kjv.3: And they come unto him, bringing one sick of the palsy, which was borne of four.
kjv.4: And when they could not come nigh unto him for the press, they uncovered the roof where he was: and when they had broken it up, they let down the bed wherein the sick of the palsy lay.
kjv.5: When Jesus saw their faith, he said unto the sick of the palsy, Son, thy sins be forgiven thee.
kjv.6: But there were certain of the scribes sitting there, and reasoning in their hearts,
kjv.7: Why doth this man thus speak blasphemies? who can forgive sins but God only?
kjv.8: And immediately when Jesus perceived in his spirit that they so reasoned within themselves, he said unto them, Why reason ye these things in your hearts?
kjv.9: Whether is it easier to say to the sick of the palsy, Thy sins be forgiven thee; or to say, Arise, and take up thy bed, and walk?
kjv.10: But that ye may know that the Son of man hath power on earth to forgive sins, (he saith to the sick of the palsy,)
kjv.11: I say unto thee, Arise, and take up thy bed, and go thy way into thine house.
kjv.12: And immediately he arose, took up the bed, and went forth before them all; insomuch that they were all amazed, and glorified God, saying, We never saw it on this fashion.
kjv.13: And he went forth again by the sea side; and all the multitude resorted unto him, and he taught them.
kjv.14: And as he passed by, he saw Levi the son of Alphaeus sitting at the receipt of custom, and said unto him, Follow me. And he arose and followed him.
kjv.15: And it came to pass, that, as Jesus sat at meat in his house, many publicans and sinners sat also together with Jesus and his disciples: for there were many, and they followed him.
kjv.16: And when the scribes and Pharisees saw him eat with publicans and sinners, they said unto his disciples, How is it that he eateth and drinketh with publicans and sinners?
kjv.17: When Jesus heard it, he saith unto them, They that are whole have no need of the physician, but they that are sick: I came not to call the righteous, but sinners to repentance.
kjv.18: And the disciples of John and of the Pharisees used to fast: and they come and say unto him, Why do the disciples of John and of the Pharisees fast, but thy disciples fast not?
kjv.19: And Jesus said unto them, Can the children of the bridechamber fast, while the bridegroom is with them? as long as they have the bridegroom with them, they cannot fast.
kjv.20: But the days will come, when the bridegroom shall be taken away from them, and then shall they fast in those days.
kjv.21: No man also seweth a piece of new cloth on an old garment: else the new piece that filled it up taketh away from the old, and the rent is made worse.
kjv.22: And no man putteth new wine into old bottles: else the new wine doth burst the bottles, and the wine is spilled, and the bottles will be marred: but new wine must be put into new bottles.
kjv.23: And it came to pass, that he went through the corn fields on the sabbath day; and his disciples began, as they went, to pluck the ears of corn.
kjv.24: And the Pharisees said unto him, Behold, why do they on the sabbath day that which is not lawful?
kjv.25: And he said unto them, Have ye never read what David did, when he had need, and was an hungred, he, and they that were with him?
kjv.26: How he went into the house of God in the days of Abiathar the high priest, and did eat the shewbread, which is not lawful to eat but for the priests, and gave also to them which were with him?
kjv.27: And he said unto them, The sabbath was made for man, and not man for the sabbath:
kjv.28: Therefore the Son of man is Lord also of the sabbath.

notes.title: Broken syntax — who is Jesus talking to mid-verse?
notes.body:
The sentence starts addressed to the scribes ("but so that you may know [ἵνα δὲ εἰδῆτε] that the Son of Man has authority…") and then breaks off — **λέγει τῷ παραλυτικῷ**, "he says to the paralytic: 'Rise…'". The ἵνα clause never gets its main verb; the narrative supplies it by *doing* it.

This is an **anacoluthon** (broken construction). Translators handle it with punctuation: most insert a dash or parenthesis — "…authority on earth to forgive sins" — *he said to the paralytic* — "I say to you, rise…". Whatever you print, don't smooth the break away entirely; the interrupted syntax is part of the drama. (Note also λέγει — a historical present at the hinge of the action.)

notes.title: οἱ υἱοὶ τοῦ νυμφῶνος — a Semitic idiom
notes.body:
Literally "the **sons of the bridechamber**" — a Semitic idiom (בְּנֵי + noun) meaning *people belonging to / characterized by* something: here, **the wedding guests** (or specifically the groom's attendants).

The translator's spectrum:

- **KJV**: "children of the bridechamber" — formal equivalence, preserves the idiom, opaque to modern readers.
- **Modern versions**: "the wedding guests" / "the friends of the bridegroom" — dynamic equivalence, communicates the referent, loses the Hebraic texture.

This is the classic formal-vs-functional trade-off in miniature. Similar "sons of X" idioms recur throughout the NT ("sons of thunder" 3:17, "sons of light," "son of perdition") — decide on a consistent policy.
